summary: All three explorability features land (hover tooltips via USERDATA_INSPECT, continuous 0–3 lone-pair slider with soft-snap and continuous fractional morph in parameterized5Domain, drag-to-reposition with VSEPR-like strain glow and snap-back). Beat 5 was retargeted to invite the user to drag an LP into the axial seat, and the closing beat references the slider. Verification passes with only pre-existing @next/next/no-img-element warnings. Two notes: Feature 1's molecule deformation is partial (only the dragged LP and its glow move; other atoms/LPs don't reposition or strain visually), and three eslint-disable-next-line suppressions were added to Artifact.tsx instead of refactoring to the derived-state pattern adopted in MoleculeScene.tsx.

findings.text: Feature 1's molecule deformation is only partly realized. During drag, applyLpOverride only updates the dragged LP's mesh + warm strain glow; other lone pairs and bonded F atoms stay fixed at their parameterized seats, so the brief's described demo moment ('F atoms get crowded, the bond angles compress, the molecule visibly resists') doesn't appear. This falls inside the brief's degraded-form allowance (strain feedback from drag position relative to stable points) and the snap-back to the nearest equatorial seat on release does demonstrate the equatorial preference, but the headline 'molecule visibly resists' beat the brief calls out as the demo moment is muted.

Three eslint-disable-next-line comments were added to Artifact.tsx (react-hooks/set-state-in-effect at lines 134 and 972, react-hooks/refs at line 519) to silence lint rather than refactor — inconsistent with the implementation report's claim that the same warning class was fixed by switching to the React derived-state pattern in MoleculeScene.tsx. Worth a follow-up pass to apply the same pattern here.
